info.title: Buckspay Facilitator & BFF API
info.description:
HTTP wire contract for the Buckspay SDK. The SDK calls these endpoints internally via `@buckspay/relayer`; this reference is for teams building their own BFF relay route or calling the facilitator from server-side code.

All facilitator endpoints require the `x-api-key` header. **The API key is a server-side secret — it must only appear in BFF or server-side code and must never be shipped to a browser bundle.** Use `createRelayRoute` and `createSignerProxyRoute` from `@buckspay/nextjs` to build compliant BFF handlers.

servers.description: Buckspay facilitator (all endpoints). Set via BUCKSPAY_FACILITATOR_URL on the server.

facilitatorUrl.description: Base URL of the Buckspay facilitator service.

tags.name: Relay
tags.description: Submit a signed Soroban authorization entry for gasless relaying to the Stellar network.

tags.name: Fee
tags.description: Quote the fee-token amount required for gas-in-token (token gas mode) before building the forward() invocation.

tags.name: Account State
tags.description: Read on-chain state for classic G-addresses and contract C-addresses.

tags.name: Onboarding
tags.description: Build and submit the sponsored onboarding transaction; deploy passkey smart-accounts and policy session-accounts.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Signer-proxy for social and email authentication. The BFF forwards these requests to the facilitator; provider secrets remain server-side.

tags.name: Swap
tags.description: Quote a gasless EVM token swap. Requires `swapChain` to be configured in the relayer.

post.description:
Submit a signed Soroban `SorobanAuthorizationEntry` (base64 XDR) to the facilitator. The facilitator wraps it in a fee-bump transaction, submits it to Stellar, and returns a `Receipt`.

In gas-in-token mode, set `feeToken` and ensure `authorizationEntryXdr` encodes the `FeeForwarder.forward()` invocation (a single host-function op that includes the actual transfer as a sub-invocation). No separate fee auth entry is needed.

**The API key is injected server-side by the BFF; the browser never sends this header directly.**

400.description: The payload was rejected by the facilitator. The `error` field contains the machine-readable code.

value.message: recipient is not in the session allowlist

502.description: Facilitator could not reach Stellar Horizon or the submission failed infrastructure-side.

post.description:
Request a fee quote for paying Soroban gas in a token instead of XLM. The facilitator simulates the calls, prices the XLM fee as a token amount, and returns the quote including the `FeeForwarder` contract address and a ledger-based expiry.

The SDK uses this quote to build the `FeeForwarder.forward()` authorization entry before calling `POST /relay` with `feeToken` set.

400.description: The calls could not be simulated or the token is not supported.

example.message: could not simulate the provided calls

502.description: Facilitator could not reach Stellar Horizon.

get.description: Returns on-chain state for a classic Stellar G-address: whether it exists, whether it has a USDC trustline, and its XLM and USDC balances. Used by the SDK to gate the onboarding flow and to display account state in UI.

parameters.name: publicKey
parameters.description: Stellar classic public key (G-address, StrKey encoded).

502.description: Facilitator could not reach Stellar Horizon.

get.description: Returns on-chain state for a passkey smart-account or policy session-account (C-address). Same response shape as the classic account endpoint. The SDK routes G-addresses to `/stellar/account/:pk` and C-addresses here.

502.description: Facilitator could not reach Stellar Horizon.

post.description:
Build the sponsor-funded onboarding transaction for a classic G-address: creates the account if needed and establishes the USDC trustline. The facilitator returns the unsigned transaction XDR for the user to sign and submit via `POST /stellar/onboard/submit`.

If the account is already fully onboarded, `nothingToDo: true` is returned and no XDR needs to be signed.

200.description: Onboarding transaction built or account already onboarded.

400.description: Could not build the onboarding transaction.

502.description: Facilitator could not reach Stellar Horizon.

post.description: Submit the user-signed onboarding transaction XDR from `POST /stellar/onboard/build`. The facilitator fee-bumps it (so the user does not need XLM for fees) and submits it to Stellar.

502.description: Facilitator could not reach Stellar Horizon.

post.description: Sponsor-deploy a passkey smart-account (secp256r1 / WebAuthn). The SDK sends the passkey's 65-byte uncompressed public key; the facilitator holds the sponsor secret, enforces the pinned Wasm hash, and returns the deployed contract address.

200.description: Contract deployed. The `address` is the new C-address of the smart-account.

503.description: Sponsor account not configured or unavailable.

post.description: Sponsor-deploy a policy-scoped session-account bound to an ed25519 root key. The SDK sends the root public key (G-address); the facilitator holds the sponsor secret, enforces the pinned Wasm hash, and returns the deployed contract address. Requires `deploySessionAccount` support in the relayer.

503.description: Sponsor account not configured or unavailable.

post.description:
Signer-proxy endpoint. The BFF (`createSignerProxyRoute({ provider: 'web3auth', ... })`) forwards this request to the facilitator's `/auth/social` with the server-held API key injected. The facilitator verifies the provider identity token, derives or recovers the user's Stellar key, and returns it.

**Never call the facilitator's `/auth/social` directly from the browser.** Use the BFF route: the SDK's `@buckspay/signers/social` posts to your same-origin BFF which proxies the call.

The request body is provider-specific (Web3Auth / web3auth verifier). The response `publicKey` is a Stellar G-address (ed25519 StrKey).

200.description: Identity verified. Returns the derived Stellar public key.

503.description: Signer proxy not configured (BUCKSPAY_FACILITATOR_URL or BUCKSPAY_FACILITATOR_API_KEY missing on the BFF server).

post.description:
Signer-proxy endpoint. The BFF (`createSignerProxyRoute({ provider: 'email', ... })`) forwards this request to the facilitator's `/auth/email`. The facilitator verifies the one-time code and returns the user's derived Stellar public key.

**Never call the facilitator's `/auth/email` directly from the browser.** Use the BFF route: the SDK's `@buckspay/signers/email` posts to your same-origin BFF which proxies the call.

The request body is provider-specific (email address + OTP code). The response `publicKey` is a Stellar G-address (ed25519 StrKey).

200.description: OTP verified. Returns the derived Stellar public key.

503.description: Signer proxy not configured (BUCKSPAY_FACILITATOR_URL or BUCKSPAY_FACILITATOR_API_KEY missing on the BFF server).

post.description:
Get a gasless EVM token swap quote. Requires the relayer to be configured with a `swapChain` (see `FacilitatorOptions`). Returns a quote ID, expected amounts, and EIP-712 typed data for the swap authorization.

**This endpoint is available only when the EVM swap rail is configured.** If `swapChain` is absent, `BuckspayClient.swap()` fails with `SWAP_FAILED`.

400.description: Swap quote failed (pair not supported, slippage too high, etc.).

example.message: pair not supported on this chain

FacilitatorApiKey.name: x-api-key
FacilitatorApiKey.description: Server-held API key. Injected by the BFF (`createRelayRoute` / `createSignerProxyRoute` from `@buckspay/nextjs`). This key must only appear in server-side code and must never be shipped to a browser bundle.

FacilitatorChain.description: Stellar network identifier used in facilitator requests.

StellarAddress.description: Stellar address: either a classic G-address or a contract C-address.

Stroops.description: A non-negative integer encoded as a decimal string to preserve precision. For Stellar token amounts this is the smallest unit with the asset's implied decimals (USDC = 7, i.e. stroops); the same string encoding also carries fees, nonces, and EVM swap amounts (which use their own token's decimals).

token.description: USDC SAC contract address (C-address). The asset being transferred.

from.description: Payer address — either a classic G-address or a smart-contract C-address.

value.description: Transfer amount in USDC stroops (7 decimal places).

authorizationEntryXdr.description: Signed `SorobanAuthorizationEntry` as base64 XDR. In gas-in-token mode this is the `FeeForwarder.forward()` entry; in sponsored mode it is the USDC `transfer` entry.

nonce.description: Authorization nonce as a decimal string. Must match the nonce used when building the unsigned entry.

signatureExpirationLedger.description: Ledger sequence number after which the authorization entry is invalid. Must be in the future when the facilitator processes the request.

feeToken.description: Optional. Present only in gas-in-token mode. Names the token used to pay the gas fee. When set, `authorizationEntryXdr` encodes the `FeeForwarder.forward()` invocation (not a bare transfer).

sessionOp.description: Optional. Marks the relay as a session management operation (`install` grants a new session key; `revoke` removes one). The facilitator relays it as a self-administered contract call on the policy account. Absent for ordinary USDC transfers.

RelayPayload.description: Signed relay request. Mirrors the SDK `RelayPayload` type from `@buckspay/core`.

token.description: Contract address of the transferred token.

chain.description: Chain on which the transaction was submitted.

transferTx.description: Transaction hash or identifier of the submitted transaction.

ledger.description: Ledger sequence number at which the transaction was included. Absent if the facilitator did not return a ledger (omitted, not null).

status.description: Human-readable status string from the facilitator (e.g. `SUCCESS`).

Receipt.description: Relay receipt returned by `POST /relay`. Mirrors the SDK `Receipt` type from `@buckspay/core`. The SDK adapter maps the facilitator's `blockNumber` field to `ledger`.

from.description: Payer address (G or C) whose account the fee will be deducted from.

token.description: Token contract address (USDC SAC) used to pay the gas fee.

calls.description: Calls to simulate for fee estimation.

args.description: Soroban `ScVal` arguments serialized as base64 XDR strings.

FeeQuoteRequest.description: Request body for `POST /fee/quote`. Calls are simulated server-side to price the XLM fee as a token amount.

forwarder.description: `FeeForwarder` contract address. The SDK uses this to build the `forward()` authorization entry.

collector.description: Address that collects the fee (G or C). Embedded in the `forward()` invocation.

token.description: Token used to pay the fee. Echoes the request `token`.

tokenAmount.description: Fee expressed in the requested token's smallest unit (USDC stroops for USDC). The SDK passes this as the `fee_amount` argument in the `forward()` invocation.

expiresAtLedger.description: Ledger sequence after which this quote expires. The SDK refuses to build a relay with an expired quote.

FeeQuote.description: Fee quote returned by `POST /fee/quote`. Mirrors the SDK `FeeQuote` type from `@buckspay/core`.

hasUsdcTrustline.description: Whether the account has an active USDC trustline.

xlmBalance.description: Native XLM balance as a decimal string (e.g. `10.5000000`). Omitted if the facilitator returned `null` or the account does not exist.

usdcBalance.description: USDC balance as a decimal string. Omitted if the account has no trustline or the facilitator returned `null`.

AccountState.description: On-chain account state returned by the `/stellar/account/:pk` and `/stellar/contract/:address` endpoints. The SDK adapter normalizes the facilitator's `nativeBalance` field to `xlmBalance` and strips null values.

publicKey.description: G-address of the account to onboard.

unsignedTxXdr.description: Unsigned onboarding transaction as base64 XDR. Present when the account is not yet fully onboarded. Sign with the user's key and submit via `POST /stellar/onboard/submit`.

nothingToDo.description: When `true`, the account is already onboarded. No XDR needs to be signed or submitted.

publicKey.description: G-address of the account being onboarded.

signedTxXdr.description: User-signed transaction XDR from the `unsignedTxXdr` field of the build response.

passkeyPublicKey.description: 65-byte uncompressed secp256r1 / WebAuthn public key, hex-encoded. Obtained from `signer.getPublicKey()` on a `@buckspay/signers/passkey` instance.

rootPublicKey.description: ed25519 root public key (G-address) that will administer the session-account. The account's `add_signer` / `remove_signer` calls must be authorized by this key.

address.description: C-address of the newly deployed smart-account.

AuthRequest.description: Provider-specific authentication payload. The BFF (`createSignerProxyRoute`) validates only that the body is a JSON object and proxies it transparently to the facilitator. Fields are provider-specific (social: `idToken` + `verifier`; email: `email` + `code`).

publicKey.description: Derived Stellar G-address for this identity. Stable across sessions for the same provider + user.

expiresAt.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) after which the derived key session should be refreshed. Omitted if the provider does not issue expiring sessions.

AuthKeyResponse.description: Successful auth response. Mirrors the SDK `AuthDetails` type from `@buckspay/core`. The response is a transparent proxy of the facilitator's body.

chain.description: EVM chain for the swap. Must match the `swapChain` configured in `buckspayFacilitator`.

merchant.description: EVM address where the buy-token is settled. For self-custody swaps this equals `payer`.

sellToken.description: EVM contract address of the sell token.

buyToken.description: EVM contract address of the buy token.

sellAmount.description: Amount to sell in the sell token's minimal units, as a decimal string.

SwapQuoteRequest.description: Request body for `POST /swap/quote`. Corresponds to the body built in `buckspayFacilitator.quoteSwap()`.

sellAmount.description: Actual sell amount (may differ from requested amount after routing).

expectedBuyAmount.description: Expected amount of buy token received.

minBuyAmount.description: Minimum buy amount after slippage. The client enforces this floor before submitting.

typedData.description: EIP-712 typed data string for the swap authorization. Required if `needsAuthorization` is `true`.

needsAuthorization.description: Whether the payer must sign the EIP-712 `typedData` before submitting the swap.

source.description: Liquidity source or aggregator used to build the quote.

expiresAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp after which the quote is invalid.

SwapQuoteResponse.description: Swap quote returned by `POST /swap/quote`. Mirrors the `swapQuoteResponseSchema` zod schema in `@buckspay/relayer`.

error.description: Machine-readable error code. SDK error codes (e.g. `RELAYER_REJECTED`, `SESSION_POLICY_VIOLATION`) or raw facilitator codes (e.g. `auth_expired`, `simulation_failed`) depending on context.

message.description: Optional human-readable detail. Never log raw upstream messages — they may contain addresses or sensitive information.

details.description: Optional structured validation errors (present on BFF-level schema failures).

ErrorResponse.description: Error body returned on non-2xx responses. The `error` field maps to a `BuckspayErrorCode` or a raw facilitator error string (see `mapFacilitatorError` in `@buckspay/relayer`).
